publication.changeSummary: Original misconception and checkpoint items retained; executable evidence is intentionally absent because Stylesheet architecture across reset, base, layout, component, and utility files requires multi-file maintainability review.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent standards, browser, and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: Human review must verify platform-version accuracy, equivalence, accessibility, and deterministic grading before formal use.
items.title: Stylesheet organization: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling misconception? Adding !important wherever a conflict appears is a scalable organization strategy.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Define ownership and cascade layers, keep specificity low, and reserve important rules for explicit policy rather than conflict repair.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Define ownership and cascade layers, keep specificity low, and reserve important rules for explicit policy rather than conflict repair.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Define ownership and cascade layers, keep specificity low, and reserve important rules for explicit policy rather than conflict repair.
Adding !important wherever a conflict appears is a scalable organization strategy.
